ops.title: cc-pushback ‚Üí cc-steer rename COMPLETE + residual cleanup DONE 2026-07-07; now at v0.15.0 (watcher retrain on experiment-at-home 0.6 since 0.15.0) / cc-transcript 13; canonical names/paths

ops.body:
**DONE 2026-07-05 ‚Äî shipped; residual cleanup DONE 2026-07-07.** Canonical now: **cc-steer on PyPI (latest v0.10.0)**, GitHub **yasyf/cc-steer** (old URL redirects), HF dataset **yasyf/cc-steer-traces** (old `cc-pushback-traces` deleted), Python module **`cc_steer`**, data dir **`~/.cc-steer/`**, code checkout **`~/Code/cc-steer`** on BOTH this machine and `yasyf@yasyf` (old `~/Code/cc-pushback` left empty + `MOVED.md` on both). DB migrated in place (`is_pushback`‚Üí`is_steering`, `complaint*`‚Üí`direction*`, view‚Üí`accepted_steering`); shared corrections ledger `source cc-pushback`‚Üí`cc-steer`; refiner emits `{action, direction}` at refine v3/opus; judge steering frame at prompt v6. **Any other memory that still says "cc-pushback"/"~/.cc-pushback"/"cc-pushback-traces" (e.g. [[cc-pushback-ecosystem-map]], [[cc-pushback-two-stage-pipeline]]) now means the cc-steer equivalent.**

**Since 0.9.0 (found 2026-07-07):** dev continued on `yasyf@yasyf` past the release ‚Äî shipped **v0.9.1** (`0a15e71` cc-family floor bump) and **v0.10.0** (`40ddf54`, breaking `feat(triage)!: adopt cc-transcript 9 model-free verdict identity`; floor now `cc-transcript>=9,<10`). Verdict identity dropped `model`: unique per `(dedup_key, role, prompt_version)`, `model` is provenance only; `triage` table gained a nullable **`canonical_key`** (null for steering). The existing `~/.cc-steer/feedback.db` needed a **manual table rebuild** to match `FeedbackStore.verdicts_ddl()` (add `canonical_key` + swap `UNIQUE(...,model)` ‚Üí 3-col unique) ‚Äî see [[cc-steer-v0.10-verdict-identity-canonical-key]].

**`yasyf@yasyf` is a CONCURRENTLY-ACTIVE committer** (found 2026-07-07): it commits to `~/Code/cc-steer` ‚Äî often on a **detached HEAD** ‚Äî and pushes to `origin/main` on its own cadence, so `main`'s tip moved twice in one hour (13d4a16 ‚Üí 05eb35b) while I worked. ALWAYS `git fetch` before assuming a tip or pushing; don't pin a SHA as "current." The remote can't push over HTTPS non-interactively from an ssh command (`could not read Username`) but pushes fine on its own; my local machine has working GitHub creds. Reattach the remote's HEAD to `main` if you find it detached, but expect the remote's automation to re-detach it.

**Residual cleanup DONE 2026-07-07:** mirrors moved local `~/.cc-pushback/mirrors` ‚Üí `~/.cc-steer/mirrors`; old local `~/.cc-pushback` retired (redundant db+dataset deleted, `MOVED.md` left); remote `~/.cc-pushback` archived ‚Üí `~/cc-pushback-remote-data-archive-2026-07-07.tar.gz` then deleted + `MOVED.md`; remote stale `cc_pushback/` module dir removed; remote git reconciled (was detached HEAD; now clean `main`); 3 superseded local branches deleted (feat/auq-wiring, feat/question-answer-wiring, recovered/feat-triage-v5). Backups kept: `~/.cc-steer/feedback.db.bak-pre-steer`, `~/.cc-steer/feedback.db.bak-pre-v9`, `~/.cc-transcript/corrections.db.bak-pre-steer`. Still open: **PyPI `cc-pushback` 0.8.1 not yet yanked** (web-UI-only; needs user's PyPI session).

The original decisions, locked by the user 2026-07-04 via AskUserQuestion:

- **New name is `cc-steer`** (NOT `cc-steering`) ‚Äî PyPI package `cc-steer`, CLI `cc-steer`, module dir `cc_pushback/` ‚Üí `cc_steer/`, internal `PUSHBACK_*` ‚Üí `STEER*`/`STEERING_*` (STEERING_CATEGORIES already exists), `Verdict.is_pushback` ‚Üí `is_steering`.
- **Migrate the local data dir** `~/.cc-pushback/` ‚Üí `~/.cc-steer/` ‚Äî back up `feedback.db` (4,134 judged rows + refinements) first, `cp` then move, repoint every cli/store default.
- **HF dataset**: create `<user>/cc-steer-traces`, push the current corpus, then **delete the old `cc-pushback-traces`** repo.
- **Full publish autonomously** ‚Äî no stop-before-publish; go through PyPI tag/publish + HF create + GitHub release.
- **No backwards-compat anywhere** (verbatim user constraint): DB renames are one-off manual SQL migrations; code uses new names with zero dual-read/shim/fallback.

Sequencing: S2 (refiner+export reword, non-breaking) lands first, then S3. See [[cc-pushback-two-stage-pipeline]] and the handoff (cc-notes 607cb1e). DB column rename `is_pushback`‚Üí`is_steering` and `refinement.complaint*`‚Üí`direction*` happen in S3's migration.
